name: barbell clean-grip front squat
instructions: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart and the barbell resting on your upper chest, with your elbows pointing forward.
Lower your body by bending your knees and pushing your hips back, as if you are sitting back into a chair.
Keep your chest up and your back straight as you lower down, making sure your knees do not go past your toes.
Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to stand back up, extending your hips and knees.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

Lower your body by bending your knees and pushing your hips back, as if you are sitting back into a chair. Keep your chest up and your back straight as you lower down, making sure your knees do not go past your toes. Continue lowering until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as you can comfortably go. Pause for a moment at the bottom, then push through your heels to stand back up, extending your hips and knees. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Axial loading under fatigue may worsen asymmetrical curves. Prefer split-stance or supported variations.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Loaded spinal flexion or heavy axial loading should be avoided. Use supported variations.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
